text: fat is 45.3g. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
carbs is 27.2g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for a nut, fat quality matters a ton.
protein is 20.2g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 18% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 30% DV, zinc at 16% DV, iron at 22% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side. portable, practical snack food because nuts score real points in life because they are easy to use. fat and texture help satiety because they can hold up better than many snack foods. still a recognisable whole-food option because that keeps them cleaner than most processed snacks.

now the downsides. calorie density is very high because that makes portion control a real issue. protein is useful but not elite because nuts help, but they are not protein stars. cost or allergy issues can limit use because practical downsides still count.
C tier. Best only in small amounts when the calories stay under control.
